title: Speed / Distance / Time (Fast Trap)
subject: math
summary: This is one of the most useful SPTG topics because it appears in direct travel, catch-up, meeting, and average-speed problems.
The math is usually simple, but the wording often causes mistakes.
The most important ideas are distance = speed × time, relative speed, average speed, and correct unit conversion.
coreConcepts: Distance = speed × time is the base of the whole topic.
Average speed is total distance divided by total time, not the average of speeds unless conditions happen to match.
When two objects move toward each other, relative speed is the sum of their speeds.
When two objects move in the same direction, relative speed is the difference of their speeds.
Round-trip average speed is not found by simply averaging the two speeds.
Always make units consistent before solving.
derive: Speed is distance per unit time, so speed = distance / time.
Rearranging again gives time = distance / speed.
Average speed is defined by total distance divided by total time.
Relative speed measures how fast the gap changes: add if objects move toward each other, subtract if they move in the same direction.
formulas: average speed = total distance / total time
relative speed (toward each other) = v1 + v2
traps: Do not average two speeds directly unless the travel times are equal.
For equal distances, use total distance / total time or the round-trip formula.
In catch-up problems, use relative speed, not total speed.
In meeting problems, use sum of speeds.
Minutes must be converted into hours if speed is in km/h.
A faster return speed does not mean the overall average is the simple midpoint.

sptgSet.question: A car travels at 60 km/h for 2 hours. How far does it travel?
A person walks 12 km in 3 hours. Find the average speed.
A bus travels 180 km at 60 km/h. How long does it take?
Two trains move toward each other at 40 km/h and 50 km/h. They are 270 km apart. When will they meet?
Two runners move in the same direction at 14 km/h and 9 km/h. If the faster runner starts 15 km behind, how long will it take to catch up?
A car goes from A to B at 60 km/h and returns at 40 km/h. Find the average speed for the whole trip.
A cyclist rides 30 km at 15 km/h and then 20 km at 10 km/h. Find the total average speed.
Two cyclists start from the same point in opposite directions at 12 km/h and 18 km/h. How far apart are they after 4 hours?

hardSet.question: A car covers the first half of a journey at 40 km/h and the second half at 60 km/h. Find the average speed for the whole journey.
A man walks to a town at 5 km/h and returns along the same route at 3 km/h. Find the average speed for the round trip.
A bus leaves at 8:00 AM at 40 km/h. Another bus leaves the same point at 10:00 AM at 60 km/h in the same direction. At what time does the second bus catch the first?
A river current is 3 km/h. A boat moves at 15 km/h in still water. Find the downstream speed.
A river current is 3 km/h. A boat moves at 15 km/h in still water. Find the upstream speed.
A car travels 240 km. If it increases its speed by 20 km/h, the trip takes 1 hour less. Find the original speed.
A cyclist travels for 2 hours at 20 km/h, then for 1.5 hours at 16 km/h. Find the average speed for the whole trip.
Two cars are 420 km apart and drive toward each other at 50 km/h and 70 km/h. After how many hours will they be 60 km apart?
A train moves at 72 km/h. How far does it travel in 25 minutes?
A person travels 6 km by walking at 4 km/h and then 30 km by bus at 36 km/h. Find the average speed for the entire trip.
